Patch Set 5:

> One problem is that the jinja templater expects values of the form:
 > {{ name }}
 > while the old templater expects values of the form:
 > ${name}
 > Consequently, all of the template files must be modified.  After
 > making that change in my local copy, it then told me that "Apply
 > Template" expects 3 arguments, but only 2 were given.  In looking
 > at TemplatingEngine, it appears that the object must be constructed
 > by passing a template location argument.  Then perhaps the newly
 > constructed object must be passed to the "Apply Template" as its
 > first argument?

Forget my comments about the template location argument; the XXX Ops cases work, while the XXX Config cases fail, and I can't see anything obviously different about them.

The comments about the macro forms still apply.

Patch Set 5:

ill change all the templates over for you and try again and see if this cleans somethings up

Patch Set 5: -Code-Review

> ill change all the templates over for you and try again and see if
 > this cleans somethings up

OK.  I changed my local copy of tests/policy/engine/*.template - that's how it went further.  That's leaving it working in the XXX Ops cases, but failing in the XXX Config cases.

Patch Set 5:

Don't ask me why, but when I replaced the lines under "Push Config Policy" with those under "Push Ops Policy", everything worked except for two of the "Get XXX" tests.  Eyeballing with the previous lines, they looked the same to me, but obviously something is different.
